acceptanceCriteria: A numeric stray positional (0, 123) is rejected as a parse-origin failure -> exit 64
Holds WITHOUT the consumer setting parse-positional-numbers:false
Registered commands, declared numeric positionals, and the $0/help path are unaffected
Tests cover both numeric and string stray tokens
description: yargs' default 'parse-positional-numbers' coerces a bare numeric token (0, 123) in argv._ to a JS number. rejectUnknownCommands only inspects STRING tokens, so numeric strays are never rejected and the command silently proceeds. Found 2026-07-22 during the lifehash-indexer migration: 'lifehash-indexer --out file:///tmp/x --resume 0' exited 0, published NOTHING, and printed nothing — the stray '0' was swallowed. Consumers currently must work around it with .parserConfiguration({'parse-positional-numbers': false}), which lifehash did (lifehash 7c8f15b). AFFECTS ALL CURRENT CONSUMERS of the helper: webble-dapp (webble 83a53ba), webble-cli (webble a7a1be1), lifehash-indexer (lifehash 7c8f15b). FIX: inspect every leftover argv._ token regardless of runtime type (coerce to string, or handle typeof 'number') so the guarantee holds without the consumer needing a parserConfiguration workaround.
title: rejectUnknownCommands misses numeric stray positionals (yargs coerces them to number)
verification: cli-kit vitest; re-verify against lifehash-indexer + the two webble CLIs
